description: Posture-aware trigger suite for agent-architect. The skill is manual-first: it should trigger on explicit project-level AI environment setup requests and decline individual agent edits, skill management, or general architecture work.

assertion_definitions.routing_verdict_correct: The response makes the right top-level call: route into agent-architect for project-level AI environment requests, and decline or redirect individual agent/skill work or non-AI architecture tasks.
assertion_definitions.invocation_posture_explicit: For routed requests, the response explicitly or implicitly operates in the skill's manual-first posture.
assertion_definitions.negative_boundary_respected: For requests that should not route to this skill, the response explains the boundary and points to the correct alternative.
assertion_definitions.next_step_explicit: The response closes with a concrete next step.

cases.name: Explicit project AI setup request
cases.prompt: Set up the AI development environment for this repo — what agents, skills, and MCP servers do we need?

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is an explicit project-level AI environment request. Should route to agent-architect.

next_step_explicit.check: Should start with Scan mode to analyze the project.

cases.name: Agent architecture audit request
cases.prompt: Audit our current agent setup — are there gaps, overlap, or agents we should remove?

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is a project-level agent architecture audit. Should route to agent-architect Audit mode.

next_step_explicit.check: Should route to Audit mode and start with inventory.

cases.name: New project AI environment design
cases.prompt: We're starting a new Django + React project. Design the complete agent and skill setup for both Claude Code and Codex.

routing_verdict_correct.check: Explicit project-level design request spanning agents, skills, and dual platform. Should route to agent-architect.

next_step_explicit.check: Should start with Scan or Design given the project info is already provided.

cases.name: CLAUDE.md overhaul with broader architecture context
cases.prompt: Redesign our CLAUDE.md and agent architecture — the current setup is messy and needs a complete rethink.

routing_verdict_correct.check: The request is about overhauling the project's AI configuration as a whole, not just editing one file. Should route to agent-architect.

next_step_explicit.check: Should start with Scan or Audit to understand current state before redesigning.

cases.name: Individual agent creation request
cases.prompt: Create a new reviewer agent with claude-code.md and codex.toml files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is individual agent creation, not project-level architecture. Should NOT route to agent-architect.

negative_boundary_respected.check: Should redirect to agent-lifecycle-manager for individual agent work.

cases.name: Skill management request
cases.prompt: Update our review skill and optimize its trigger description.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is skill lifecycle work, not agent architecture. Should NOT route to agent-architect.

cases.name: General software architecture question
cases.prompt: Should we use a microservices or monolithic architecture for our new payment system?

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is software architecture, not AI development environment design. Should NOT route to agent-architect.

negative_boundary_respected.check: Should redirect to future-aware-architecture or handle directly.

cases.name: Simple CLAUDE.md edit without architecture context
cases.prompt: Add our test commands to CLAUDE.md.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is a simple file edit, not a project-level AI environment design. Should NOT route to agent-architect.

negative_boundary_respected.check: Should just edit the file directly without invoking the full architect workflow.

cases.name: MCP server recommendation without broader architecture context
cases.prompt: What MCP servers should I add for this PostgreSQL + Redis project?

routing_verdict_correct.check: Borderline case. Could be handled by agent-architect (MCP is part of its scope) or directly. Routing to agent-architect is acceptable if it scopes down to MCP-only advice.

next_step_explicit.check: Should either give MCP advice directly or scope down the agent-architect workflow to MCP recommendations only.

cases.name: Agent validation request that could be individual or library-wide
cases.prompt: Are our agents set up correctly? Validate them.

routing_verdict_correct.check: Borderline. Could be agent-architect Audit (library-wide) or agent-lifecycle-manager Validate (individual). Both are defensible; the response should pick one and justify.

next_step_explicit.check: Should clarify scope or pick the most reasonable interpretation.

scoring.notes: Agent-architect is manual-first. The trigger boundary should be tight: project-level AI environment work routes in, individual agent/skill work routes out. Near-miss cases allow flexibility but should show clear reasoning.

runner_instructions.setup: Ensure the skill is discoverable. These are classification-only prompts — no file edits needed.
runner_instructions.execution: Run each prompt with the skill available. The response should classify routing correctly.
runner_instructions.evaluation: Score routing verdict, boundary clarity, and next-step guidance.
